Current Report (@Currentreport1)

BREAKING:

Kim Jong-un has won North Korea's parliamentary elections with 99.93% of the vote.

Video

I feel the need to clarify that they're only "cheap" in the sense that you didn't pay for them!


Jesse Kelly (@JesseKellyDC)

I already have a Panama hat. I have cheap cigars. I got them from @michaeljknowles. I have a Hawaiian shirt my wife hates.

 I’m ready to go undercover in Cuba.



— https://nitter.net/JesseKellyDC/status/2033985013666992555#m

All news media purport to tell the truth. The difference between the old and "new" media has nothing to do with their promises to viewers. The difference involves incentives.

In the old media, the necessity of networks and geographic locality meant that broadcasters had an incentive to appeal to a majority or at least plurality of real political communities. Broadcasters also had to remain within the norms of networks, on which they relied for their reach. If a broadcaster said something beyond

👍👍👍🇺🇸🇺🇸🇺🇸


Polymarket (@Polymarket)

BREAKING: Trump says he will not endorse any Republican senator who votes against the SAVE America Act.



— https://nitter.net/Polymarket/status/2033922991193473392#m

If recent reports are to be believed, Mr. Larijani relented.


Ali Larijani | علی لاریجانی (@alilarijani_ir)

Trump says he is looking for a speedy victory. While starting a war is easy, it cannot be won with a few tweets. We will not relent until making you sorry for this grave miscalculation. 
#TrumpMustPay



— https://nitter.net/alilarijani_ir/status/2032128509817037247#m

This is the way.

👍👍👍🇺🇸🇺🇸🇺🇸


Tyler Bowyer (@tylerbowyer)

While podcasters argue on X over generally predisposed animosity towards one another, (not really about news of the day,) our team is registering voters and chasing ballots to protect the nation’s largest utility company in a swing state. 

Losing it would make it dramatically harder to win the Presidency again. 

Proud of our team!



— https://nitter.net/tylerbowyer/status/2033706533557833837#m
